---
title: "Peer Review Three"
output: 
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: columns
    vertical_layout: fill
runtime: shiny
---

<!-- Link do aplikacji: https://katarzyna-wesolowska.shinyapps.io/Wesolowska_Projekt_Dashboard/#section-page-3 -->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(flexdashboard)
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)
library(shiny)

dat <- read_csv(url("https://www.dropbox.com/s/4ebgnkdhhxo5rac/cel_volden_wiseman%20_coursera.csv?raw=1"))

dat <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    Party = recode(dem, `1` = "Democrat", `0` = "Republican"),
    Party = factor(Party, levels = c("Democrat", "Republican"))
  )

party_cols <- c("Democrat" = "blue", "Republican" = "red")
```

Page 1
======

### Chart A

```{r}
dat %>% 
  drop_na(year, Party, all_pass) %>% 
  filter(year > 1979) %>% 
  group_by(year, Party) %>% 
  summarise(passed = sum(all_pass, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = passed, fill = Party)) +
  geom_area(position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = party_cols, breaks = c("Democrat", "Republican")) +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Bills Passed Since 1980",
    x = "Year",
    y = "All Bills Passed",
    fill = "Party"
  )
```

Page 2
======

Column {data-width=500}
-------------------------------------

### Chart B

```{r}
p_b <- dat %>%
  drop_na(congress, votepct, all_pass, Party, thomas_name) %>%
  filter(congress == 110) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = votepct, y = all_pass, color = Party, fill = Party)) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      text = paste0(
        "votepct: ", votepct,
        "<br>all_pass: ", all_pass,
        "<br>thomas_name: ", thomas_name,
        "<br>Party: ", Party
      )
    ),
    size = 1.6
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess",
    formula = y ~ x,
    se = TRUE,
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = party_cols) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = party_cols) +
  labs(
    title = "Passage and Vote Pct., 110th Congress",
    x = "Vote Pct.",
    y = "All Pass",
    color = "Party",
    fill = "Party"
  )

ggplotly(p_b, tooltip = "text")
```

Column {data-width=500}
-------------------------------------

### Chart C

```{r}
p_c <- dat %>%
  drop_na(congress, dwnom1, all_pass, Party, thomas_name) %>%
  filter(congress == 110) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = dwnom1, y = all_pass, color = Party)) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      text = paste0(
        "DW Nominate: ", dwnom1,
        "<br>all_pass: ", all_pass,
        "<br>thomas_name: ", thomas_name,
        "<br>Party: ", Party
      )
    )
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    formula = y ~ x,
    se = TRUE,
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = party_cols) +
  labs(
    title = "Passage and Ideology, 110th Congress",
    x = "DW Nominate",
    y = "All Pass",
    color = "Party"
  )

ggplotly(p_c, tooltip = "text")
```

Page 3
======

Sidebar {.sidebar}
-------------------------------------

```{r}
selectizeInput(
  inputId = "states",
  label = "State",
  choices = sort(unique(dat$st_name)),
  selected = sort(unique(dat$st_name)),
  multiple = TRUE,
  options = list(plugins = list("remove_button"))
)
```

Column
-------------------------------------

### Chart D

```{r}
renderPlot({
  req(input$states)
  
  dat %>% 
    filter(congress == 110, st_name %in% input$states) %>%
    group_by(st_name) %>%
    summarise(passed = sum(all_pass, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = st_name, y = passed)) +
    geom_col(fill = "gray35") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(
      title = "Total Bills Passed by State Delegations, 110th Congress",
      x = "State Name",
      y = "Total Bills Passed Per State"
    )
}, height = 750)
```
